qtype: algebraic simplification, substitution, rearranging formula, solving simultaneous linear equations
tex:
(a) Simplify

(i) (b) $5a - 6a + 3a$

(c) $6x^{2} - 6x - 4x^{2} - x$

(d) Find the value of $c^{2} + d^{2}$ when $c = 7$ and $d = -5$.
\newline

(e) The time, $T$ minutes, to cook a chicken with a mass of $m$ kg is $T = 35m + 20$.
\newline

(i) (f) Make $m$ the subject of the formula.
\newline

(g) Find the mass of a chicken that takes 83 minutes to cook.
\newline

(h) Solve these simultaneous equations.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

$$5x - 6y = 24$$
$$15x + 8y = 33$$

tex:
(a) Anton records the number of pets owned by each of 50 families.

\newline

\newline

\newline

(d) Write down the mode.
\newline

(e) Calculate the mean.
\newline

\newline

The table shows the number of each pet.
\newline

(i) (g) Complete the table.
\newline

(h) Complete the pie chart.
\newline

(i) One of the pets is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that a rabbit is chosen.
\newline

\newline

(h) Write down the coordinates of the points where this line intersects the graph of

$y = -x^{2} + 8x + 1$.
\newline

tex:
The area of some land is in the ratio park : gardens : playground = 11 : 2 : 3.
\newline

The park has an area of 4620 m$^{2}$.
\newline

(a) Work out the area of the gardens and the area of the playground.
\newline

(b) The park area of 4620 m$^{2}$ is made up of paths and grassland.
\newline

18\% of the park area is paths.
\newline

(i) (c) Show that the grassland area is 3788.4 m$^{2}$.
\newline

(d) Seed for the grassland is sold in bags.
\newline

The seed in one bag covers an area of 280 m$^{2}$.
\newline

The bags cost \$72 each for the first 5 bags and then \$58 each for any extra bags.
\newline

Calculate the cost of the seed needed to cover the grassland.
\newline

(e) The owners of the land buy new equipment for the playground.
\newline

They borrow \$8500 for 4 years at a rate of 6.5\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the amount they repay at the end of the 4 years.
\newline

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

(f) The café in the park sells water in bottles A, B and C.
\newline

Work out which bottle is the best value.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

content.content:
Work out the area of the gardens and the area of the playground.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

The seed in one bag covers an area of 280 m$^{2}$.
\newline

The bags cost \$72 each for the first 5 bags and then \$58 each for any extra bags.
\newline

\newline

The owners of the land buy new equipment for the playground.
\newline

They borrow \$8500 for 4 years at a rate of 6.5\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the amount they repay at the end of the 4 years.
\newline

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

The café in the park sells water in bottles A, B and C.
\newline

Work out which bottle is the best value.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

stem.content:
The area of some land is in the ratio park : gardens : playground = 11 : 2 : 3.
\newline

The park has an area of 4620 m$^{2}$.
\newline

_rawBody:
The area of some land is in the ratio park : gardens : playground = 11 : 2 : 3.

The park has an area of 4620 m$^{2}$.

\begin{parts}
\item Work out the area of the gardens and the area of the playground.

\AnswerLine[Gardens][m$^{2}$]

\vgap

\AnswerLine[Playground][m$^{2}$]
\Marks{3}

\item The park area of 4620 m$^{2}$ is made up of paths and grassland.

18\% of the park area is paths.

\begin{subparts}
\item Show that the grassland area is 3788.4 m$^{2}$.

\Answerspace[3cm]
\Marks{1}

\item Seed for the grassland is sold in bags.

The seed in one bag covers an area of 280 m$^{2}$.

The bags cost \$72 each for the first 5 bags and then \$58 each for any extra bags.

\vgap

Calculate the cost of the seed needed to cover the grassland.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{4}
\end{subparts}

\item The owners of the land buy new equipment for the playground.

They borrow \$8500 for 4 years at a rate of 6.5\% per year compound interest.

\vgap

Calculate the amount they repay at the end of the 4 years.

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item The café in the park sells water in bottles A, B and C.
\relinput{Figures/Q07d.tex}

\vgap

Work out which bottle is the best value.

You must show all your working.

\AnswerLine[Bottle][]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

tex:
The diagram shows a plan, ABCDE, of the floor of a room in Jo's house.
\newline

F is a point inside the room.
\newline

(a) (i) (b) Show that EF = 1.9 m.
\newline

(c) Work out $AF$.
\newline

(d) Calculate the area of the floor.
\newline

(e) A cupboard in the room is in the shape of a cuboid.
\newline

The area of the base of the cupboard is 1.2 m$^{2}$ and the height of the cupboard is 2.3 m.
\newline

Calculate the volume of the cupboard.
\newline

Give the units of your answer.
\newline

  ~ \DotLine{10}

(f) Jo buys 275 floor tiles which cost \$1.64 each.
\newline

Calculate the total cost of the floor tiles.
\newline

(g) Jo builds a patio in the shape of a semicircle with radius 2.3 m.
\newline

Calculate the area of the patio.
\newline

qtype: speed and unit conversion, average speed calculation with rest stop
tex:
(a) Sara rides her bicycle at a speed of 420 metres per minute.
\newline

Work out her speed in kilometres per hour.
\newline

(b) Jan cycles a distance of 51 km.
\newline

She starts at 11 55.
\newline

She has a rest stop for 25 minutes.
\newline

She finishes at 14 41.
\newline

Calculate her average speed, in km/h, for the time she is cycling.
\newline

content.content:
Sara rides her bicycle at a speed of 420 metres per minute.
\newline

Work out her speed in kilometres per hour.
\newline

Jan cycles a distance of 51 km.
\newline

She starts at 11 55.
\newline

She has a rest stop for 25 minutes.
\newline

She finishes at 14 41.
\newline

Calculate her average speed, in km/h, for the time she is cycling.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item Sara rides her bicycle at a speed of 420 metres per minute.

\vgap

Work out her speed in kilometres per hour.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{2}

\item Jan cycles a distance of 51 km.

She starts at 11 55.

She has a rest stop for 25 minutes.

She finishes at 14 41.

\vgap

Calculate her average speed, in km/h, for the time she is cycling.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{4}

\end{parts}
